ncert_truth_statements: The EMF of a cell is the work done per unit charge by the cell to move charge from its negative terminal to its positive terminal through the cell
The terminal voltage of a cell during discharge is V = E − Ir, which is less than the EMF because of the potential drop across the internal resistance
When no current is drawn from the cell (open circuit), the terminal voltage equals the EMF of the cell
The internal resistance of a cell arises from the resistance of the electrolyte and electrodes inside the cell
During charging, the terminal voltage across the cell is greater than its EMF: V = E + Ir
